verses.text: Lazarus of Bethany is sick; his sisters send, 'he whom thou lovest is sick.' Need to Christ. They bring their trouble to the Lord who loves them.
Lazarus of Bethany is sick; his sisters send, 'he whom thou lovest is sick.' Need to Christ. They bring their trouble to the Lord who loves them.
Lazarus of Bethany is sick; his sisters send, 'he whom thou lovest is sick.' Need to Christ. They bring their trouble to the Lord who loves them.
This sickness is 'for the glory of God'; yet He abides two days where He was. Loving delay. He waits, that His glory may be greater.
This sickness is 'for the glory of God'; yet He abides two days where He was. Loving delay. He waits, that His glory may be greater.
This sickness is 'for the glory of God'; yet He abides two days where He was. Loving delay. He waits, that His glory may be greater.
Let us go to Judea; they warn of the Jews; 'are there not twelve hours in the day?' Fearless. He walks safely in the path of duty.
Let us go to Judea; they warn of the Jews; 'are there not twelve hours in the day?' Fearless. He walks safely in the path of duty.
Let us go to Judea; they warn of the Jews; 'are there not twelve hours in the day?' Fearless. He walks safely in the path of duty.
Let us go to Judea; they warn of the Jews; 'are there not twelve hours in the day?' Fearless. He walks safely in the path of duty.
Lazarus sleeps; I go to wake him; they think He means rest. Misunderstood. Death is but a sleep from which Christ can wake.
Lazarus sleeps; I go to wake him; they think He means rest. Misunderstood. Death is but a sleep from which Christ can wake.
Lazarus sleeps; I go to wake him; they think He means rest. Misunderstood. Death is but a sleep from which Christ can wake.
'Lazarus is dead... I am glad... to the intent ye may believe'; Thomas, Let us also go and die with him. Loyal but gloomy. Thomas would follow even to death.
'Lazarus is dead... I am glad... to the intent ye may believe'; Thomas, Let us also go and die with him. Loyal but gloomy. Thomas would follow even to death.
'Lazarus is dead... I am glad... to the intent ye may believe'; Thomas, Let us also go and die with him. Loyal but gloomy. Thomas would follow even to death.
Lazarus has lain four days; Martha meets Him, but Mary sits still. Two sisters. Grief draws them differently to Christ.
Lazarus has lain four days; Martha meets Him, but Mary sits still. Two sisters. Grief draws them differently to Christ.
Lazarus has lain four days; Martha meets Him, but Mary sits still. Two sisters. Grief draws them differently to Christ.
Lazarus has lain four days; Martha meets Him, but Mary sits still. Two sisters. Grief draws them differently to Christ.
'Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died'; he shall rise again. Faith with regret. She believes, yet wishes He had come sooner.
'Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died'; he shall rise again. Faith with regret. She believes, yet wishes He had come sooner.
'Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died'; he shall rise again. Faith with regret. She believes, yet wishes He had come sooner.
'Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died'; he shall rise again. Faith with regret. She believes, yet wishes He had come sooner.
'I am the resurrection, and the life... whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die'; she answers, 'I believe.' Life in Christ. He is Himself the conqueror of death.
'I am the resurrection, and the life... whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die'; she answers, 'I believe.' Life in Christ. He is Himself the conqueror of death.
'I am the resurrection, and the life... whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die'; she answers, 'I believe.' Life in Christ. He is Himself the conqueror of death.
Mary comes, falls at His feet, 'Lord, if thou hadst been here.' Same sorrow. She, like Martha, brings her grief and faith to Him.
Mary comes, falls at His feet, 'Lord, if thou hadst been here.' Same sorrow. She, like Martha, brings her grief and faith to Him.
Mary comes, falls at His feet, 'Lord, if thou hadst been here.' Same sorrow. She, like Martha, brings her grief and faith to Him.
Mary comes, falls at His feet, 'Lord, if thou hadst been here.' Same sorrow. She, like Martha, brings her grief and faith to Him.
Mary comes, falls at His feet, 'Lord, if thou hadst been here.' Same sorrow. She, like Martha, brings her grief and faith to Him.
He groans, troubled; 'Where have ye laid him?' 'Jesus wept.' True tears. The Lord of life weeps with the mourning.
He groans, troubled; 'Where have ye laid him?' 'Jesus wept.' True tears. The Lord of life weeps with the mourning.
He groans, troubled; 'Where have ye laid him?' 'Jesus wept.' True tears. The Lord of life weeps with the mourning.
'Behold how he loved him'; could not He have kept him from dying? Doubting murmur. They question the love they have just seen weep.
'Behold how he loved him'; could not He have kept him from dying? Doubting murmur. They question the love they have just seen weep.
'Behold how he loved him'; could not He have kept him from dying? Doubting murmur. They question the love they have just seen weep.
'Take ye away the stone'; he is four days dead; 'if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God.' Faith first. Belief opens the eyes to God's glory.
'Take ye away the stone'; he is four days dead; 'if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God.' Faith first. Belief opens the eyes to God's glory.
'Father, I thank thee'; 'Lazarus, come forth'; the dead man comes out bound; 'Loose him.' Word of power. At His call the grave gives up its dead.
'Father, I thank thee'; 'Lazarus, come forth'; the dead man comes out bound; 'Loose him.' Word of power. At His call the grave gives up its dead.
'Father, I thank thee'; 'Lazarus, come forth'; the dead man comes out bound; 'Loose him.' Word of power. At His call the grave gives up its dead.
'Father, I thank thee'; 'Lazarus, come forth'; the dead man comes out bound; 'Loose him.' Word of power. At His call the grave gives up its dead.
Many believe; others tell the Pharisees; if we let Him alone, all will believe. Hardened by a miracle. The very wonder hardens His foes.
Many believe; others tell the Pharisees; if we let Him alone, all will believe. Hardened by a miracle. The very wonder hardens His foes.
Many believe; others tell the Pharisees; if we let Him alone, all will believe. Hardened by a miracle. The very wonder hardens His foes.
Many believe; others tell the Pharisees; if we let Him alone, all will believe. Hardened by a miracle. The very wonder hardens His foes.
Caiaphas says it is expedient one die for the people; he prophesied Jesus should die for the nation. Unwitting truth. The high priest speaks better than he knew.

Caiaphas says it is expedient one die for the people; he prophesied Jesus should die for the nation. Unwitting truth. The high priest speaks better than he knew.
They plot to kill Him; He walks no more openly; men seek Him at the passover. Hour drawing near. He withdraws till the appointed time.
